content:
<USER_REQUEST>
Create 3 prediction papers each for 4 subjects. Save them in the Predictions folder under each subject directory.

## Subject 1: Database Systems (BCSE302L)
Path: D:\Study Papers\Joel\Database_Systems\Predictions\
Topics: ER/EER diagrams, relational algebra, SQL DDL/DML, normalization (1NF-BCNF), functional dependencies, B+ tree indexing, concurrency control (2PL, timestamp ordering), ACID properties, transaction management, recovery techniques (log-based, checkpointing), query optimization, relational calculus
Format: CAT = 5×10=50 marks. FAT = 10×10=100 marks.

## Subject 2: Discrete Mathematics and Graph Theory (BMAT205L)
Path: D:\Study Papers\Joel\Discrete_Mathematics_and_Graph_Theory\Predictions\
Topics: Mathematical logic (propositional & predicate), sets/relations/functions, lattices, algebraic structures (groups, subgroups, rings), combinatorics, pigeonhole principle, inclusion-exclusion, recurrence relations, generating functions, graph theory (Euler/Hamiltonian circuits, graph coloring, chromatic number, planar graphs, trees, spanning trees, Kruskal/Prim)
Format: CAT = 5×10=50 marks (4-credit course). FAT = 10×10=100 marks.

## Subject 3: Cloud Computing (BMEE355E)
Path: D:\Study Papers\Joel\Cloud_Computing\Predictions\
From actual CAT-2 paper (Oct 2024 - CSI3001), I observed: Q1 used MAX-MIN and SJF scheduling algorithms with VMs, Q2 was a scenario-based healthcare company data migration challenge.
Topics: Cloud service models (IaaS/PaaS/SaaS), virtualization types, hypervisors, VM scheduling (MAX-MIN, SJF), cloud security, deployment models, MapReduce, containers/Docker, SLAs, cloud storage, load balancing
Format: CAT = 5×10=50 marks. FAT = 10×10=100 marks.

## Subject 4: Digital Marketing (BMGT106L)
Path: D:\Study Papers\Joel\Digital_Marketing\Predictions\
Topics: Digital marketing landscape, SEO/SEM, social media marketing, content marketing, email marketing, PPC advertising, Google Analytics, marketing automation, conversion rate optimization, influencer marketing, brand management online, digital marketing strategy & planning
Format: CAT = 5×10=50 marks. FAT = 10×10=100 marks.

Also create:
## Subject 5: Indian Constitution (BSSC102N)
Path: D:\Study Papers\Joel\Indian_Constitution\Predictions\
Topics: Preamble, Fundamental Rights (Art 14-32), Directive Principles, Fundamental Duties, Union/State Legislature, Executive, Judiciary, Emergency provisions, Constitutional amendments, Local self-government, Election Commission
Format: CAT = 5×10=50 marks. FAT = 10×10=100 marks. (OC = 2 credit online course)

Create 3 files per subject (CAT1, CAT2, FAT). Match VIT paper format exactly with header, table format, CO/BL columns.
</USER_REQUEST>
<ADDITIONAL_METADATA>
The current local time is: 2026-06-14T18:42:25+05:30.
</ADDITIONAL_METADATA>

13: 
14: ## 📋 Exam Pattern
15: | Exam | Weight | Duration | Format |
16: |------|--------|----------|--------|

18: | CAT-2 | ~15% | 1.5 hrs | Theory |

22: ## 🎯 Key Topics (Based on PYQ Analysis)
23: 
24: ### Module 1: Mathematical Logic
25: - Statements & Connectives
26: - Truth Tables & Logical Equivalences
27: - Tautology, Contradiction, Contingency
28: - Inference Theory & Predicate Logic
29: 
30: ### Module 2: Relations, Functions & Lattices
31: - Binary Relations & Properties
32: - Equivalence Relations & Partitions
33: - Partial Orders & Hasse Diagrams
34: - Lattice Properties
35: 
36: ### Module 3: Algebraic Structures & Combinatorics
37: - Groups, Semi-groups, Monoids
38: - Subgroups & Homomorphisms
39: - Permutations & Combinations
40: - Pigeonhole Principle
41: 
42: ### Module 4: Recurrence Relations
43: - Generating Functions
44: - Solving Homogeneous & Non-Homogeneous Recurrences
45: - Characteristic Equation Method
46: 
47: ### Module 5: Graphs and Trees
48: - Graph Types & Properties
49: - Euler & Hamiltonian Graphs
50: - Graph Coloring (Chromatic Number)
51: - Planar Graphs & Kuratowski's Theorem
52: - Trees & Spanning Trees (Kruskal, Prim)
53: 
54: ## 📖 Recommended Textbooks

"# VELLORE INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, CHENNAI

## SCHOOL OF COMPUTER SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING (SCOPE)
### CONTINUOUS ASSESSMENT TEST - I
### FALL SEMESTER 2026-27

---

| | |
|---|---|
| **Programme Name & Branch** | B.Tech (CSE) |
| **Course Code and Course Name** | BCSE302L – Database Systems |
| **Slot** | — |
| **Class Number(s)** | — |
| **Faculty Name(s)** | — |
| **Date of Examination** | — |
| **Exam Duration** | 90 minutes |
| **Maximum Marks** | **50** |

---

### General Instructions:
- Answer **ALL** questions.
- M – Max mark; CO – Course Outcome; BL – Bloom's Taxonomy Level  
  (1 – Remember, 2 – Understand, 3 – Apply, 4 – Analyse, 5 – Evaluate, 6 – Create)

### Course Outcomes:
- **CO1** – Understand the fundamentals of database systems and data models
- **CO2** – Apply relational algebra and SQL for data manipulation
- **CO3** – Analyse functional dependencies and apply normalization techniques

---

### Answer ALL Questions (5 × 10 = 50 Marks)

| Q. No | Question | M | CO | BL |
|:-----:|----------|:-:|:--:|:--:|
| 1. | **(a)** Explain the three-schema architecture of a database system with a neat diagram. Discuss how data independence is achieved at each level. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Differentiate between database schema and database state with suitable examples. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO1 | BL2 |
| 2. | Consider the following university database: | | | |
| | **STUDENT** (Roll_No, Name, DOB, Dept_Name) | | | |
| | **COURSE** (Course_ID, Course_Name, Credits, Dept_Name) | | | |
| | **ENROLLMENT** (Roll_No, Course_ID, Semester, Grade) | | | |
| | **DEPARTMENT** (Dept_Name, HOD_Name, Building) | | | |
| | **(a)** Draw the ER diagram for the above schema showing entities, attributes (identifying primary keys and partial keys), relationships with cardinality constraints, and participation constraints. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Extend the above ER diagram to an EER diagram by introducing: (i) a specialization

### Course Outcomes:
- **CO3** – Analyse functional dependencies and apply normalization techniques
- **CO4** – Apply indexing and query optimization techniques
- **CO5** – Understand transaction management, concurrency control, and recovery

---

### Answer ALL Questions (5 × 10 = 50 Marks)

| Q. No | Question | M | CO | BL |
|:-----:|----------|:-:|:--:|:--:|
| 1. | **(a)** Construct a B+ tree of order 4 (maximum 3 keys per node) by inserting the following keys in the given order: **5, 8, 1, 7, 3, 12, 9, 6, 15, 11, 2, 10**. Show the tree after each split operation. **(6 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Now delete keys **8** and **3** from the constructed B+ tree. Show the tree after each deletion, indicating any merging or redistribution that occurs. **(4 Marks)** | 10 | CO4 | BL3 |
| 2. | **(a)** Explain the ACID properties of a transaction with suitable examples. Why is each property important for maintaining database consistency? **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Consider the following schedule S of three transactions: | | | |
| | `S: R1(A), R2(A), W1(A), R3(B), W2(B), R1(B), W3(A), W1(B), Commit1, Commit2, Commit3` | | | |
| | (i) Draw the precedence graph for schedule S. **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Is the schedule conflict-serializable? If yes, find all equivalent s

args.CodeContent:
"# VELLORE INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, CHENNAI

## SCHOOL OF COMPUTER SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING (SCOPE)
### FINAL ASSESSMENT TEST (FAT)
### FALL SEMESTER 2026-27

---

| | |
|---|---|
| **Programme Name & Branch** | B.Tech (CSE) |
| **Course Code and Course Name** | BCSE302L – Database Systems |
| **Slot** | — |
| **Class Number(s)** | — |
| **Faculty Name(s)** | — |
| **Date of Examination** | — |
| **Exam Duration** | 180 minutes (3 Hours) |
| **Maximum Marks** | **100** |

---

### General Instructions:
- Answer **ALL** questions.
- M – Max mark; CO – Course Outcome; BL – Bloom's Taxonomy Level  
  (1 – Remember, 2 – Understand, 3 – Apply, 4 – Analyse, 5 – Evaluate, 6 – Create)

### Course Outcomes:
- **CO1** – Understand the fundamentals of database systems and data models
- **CO2** – Apply relational algebra and SQL for data manipulation
- **CO3** – Analyse functional dependencies and apply normalization techniques
- **CO4** – Apply indexing and query optimization techniques
- **CO5** – Understand transaction management, concurrency control, and recovery

---

### Answer ALL Questions (10 × 10 = 100 Marks)

| Q. No | Question | M | CO | BL |
|:-----:|----------|:-:|:--:|:--:|
| 1. | **(a)** Explain the concept of data models in DBMS. Compare and contrast the Hierarchical, Network, and Relational data models with examples. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Discuss the roles of DBA and end-users in a database environment. Explain the different types of database users with their interactions. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO1 | BL2 |
| 2. | A hospital management system has the following requirements: | | | |
| | - A **DOCTOR** has Doctor_ID, Name, Specialization, and Phone. | | | |
| | - A **PATIENT** has Patient_ID, Name, DOB, Address, and Blood_Group. | | | |
| | - A **WARD** has Ward_No, Ward_Type, and Capacity. | | | |
| | - Doctors treat patients; each treatment has a Date and Diagnosis. | | | |
